articles.title: Jay Chou album and K-pop tours headline Tencent Music’s Q1
articles.summary: Tencent Music (TME) reported a 7.3% year-over-year increase in total revenue to RMB7.90 billion for Q1 2026, driven by strong growth in music-related services. While non-IFRS net profit rose by 7.0%, IFRS net profit declined due to a prior-year disposal gain. The company emphasized its continued investment in premium IP, expansion of membership privileges, and strategic collaborations, including a digital album with Jay Chou and K-pop artist tours, to engage users and drive monetization.
articles.content: Tencent Music (TME) reported a 7.3% year-over-year increase in total revenue to RMB7.90 billion for Q1 2026, driven by strong growth in music-related services. While non-IFRS net profit rose by 7.0%, IFRS net profit declined due to a prior-year disposal gain. The company emphasized its continued investment in premium IP, expansion of membership privileges, and strategic collaborations, including a digital album with Jay Chou and K-pop artist tours, to engage users and drive monetization.

articles.title: Agilent expands collaboration with C-CAMP to scale access to advanced life sciences analytics
articles.summary: Agilent Technologies has expanded its collaboration with India's Centre for Cellular and Molecular Platforms (C-CAMP) to increase access to advanced analytical and mass spectrometry capabilities for life sciences and therapeutic research. This partnership will provide researchers from startups, academia, and industry with high-resolution analytical platforms via C-CAMP's shared infrastructure, eliminating the need for direct capital investment in specialized instruments. The initiative aims to reduce barriers and support reproducible science, thereby accelerating next-generation biotherapeutic
articles.content: Agilent Technologies has expanded its collaboration with India's Centre for Cellular and Molecular Platforms (C-CAMP) to increase access to advanced analytical and mass spectrometry capabilities for life sciences and therapeutic research. This partnership will provide researchers from startups, academia, and industry with high-resolution analytical platforms via C-CAMP's shared infrastructure, eliminating the need for direct capital investment in specialized instruments. The initiative aims to reduce barriers and support reproducible science, thereby accelerating next-generation biotherapeutics and strengthening India's position as a global biopharma hub.
